lean_translation:
def linear_search (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : Int :=
  match arr.indexOf? target with
  | some i => i
  | none => -1

theorems.name: linear_search_not_found
theorems.statement: theorem linear_search_not_found (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : linear_search arr target = -1 → target ∉ arr

theorems.name: linear_search_found_valid
theorems.statement: theorem linear_search_found_valid (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (h : linear_search arr target ≠ -1) : 0 ≤ linear_search arr target ∧ linear_search arr target < arr.length

theorems.name: linear_search_nil
theorems.statement: theorem linear_search_nil (target : Int) : linear_search [] target = -1

lean_translation:
partial def binary_search (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : Int :=
  let rec loop (lo hi : Int) : Int :=
    if lo > hi then -1
    else
      let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2
      match arr.get? mid.toNat with
      | some x =>
        if x == target then mid
        else if x < target then loop (mid + 1) hi
        else loop lo (mid - 1)
      | none => -1
  loop 0 (arr.length - 1)

theorems.name: binary_search_found_valid
theorems.statement: theorem binary_search_found_valid (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (h : binary_search arr target ≠ -1) : 0 ≤ binary_search arr target ∧ binary_search arr target < arr.length

theorems.name: binary_search_nil
theorems.statement: theorem binary_search_nil (target : Int) : binary_search [] target = -1

theorems.name: binary_search_found_eq
theorems.statement: theorem binary_search_found_eq (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (h : binary_search arr target ≠ -1) : arr.get? (binary_search arr target).toNat = some target

lean_translation:
partial def find_first (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : Int :=
  let rec loop (lo hi result : Int) : Int :=
    if lo > hi then result
    else
      let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2
      match arr.get? mid.toNat with
      | some x =>
        if x == target then loop lo (mid - 1) mid
        else if x < target then loop (mid + 1) hi result
        else loop lo (mid - 1) result
      | none => result
  loop 0 (arr.length - 1) (-1)

theorems.name: find_first_le_find_last
theorems.statement: theorem find_first_le_find_last (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : find_first arr target ≤ find_last arr target ∨ find_first arr target = -1

theorems.name: find_first_is_first
theorems.statement: theorem find_first_is_first (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (i : Nat) : find_first arr target ≠ -1 → (i : Int) < find_first arr target → 0 ≤ i → arr.get? i.toNat ≠ some target

lean_translation:
partial def find_last (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : Int :=
  let rec loop (lo hi result : Int) : Int :=
    if lo > hi then result
    else
      let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2
      match arr.get? mid.toNat with
      | some x =>
        if x == target then loop (mid + 1) hi mid
        else if x < target then loop (mid + 1) hi result
        else loop lo (mid - 1) result
      | none => result
  loop 0 (arr.length - 1) (-1)

theorems.name: find_last_is_last
theorems.statement: theorem find_last_is_last (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (i : Int) : find_last arr target ≠ -1 → find_last arr target < i → i < arr.length → arr.get? i.toNat ≠ some target

theorems.name: find_last_found_eq
theorems.statement: theorem find_last_found_eq (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (h : find_last arr target ≠ -1) : arr.get? (find_last arr target).toNat = some target

lean_translation:
def contains (arr : List Int) (val : Int) : Bool :=
  arr.any (· == val)

theorems.name: contains_iff_mem
theorems.statement: theorem contains_iff_mem (arr : List Int) (val : Int) : contains arr val = true ↔ val ∈ arr

theorems.name: contains_nil
theorems.statement: theorem contains_nil (val : Int) : contains [] val = false

theorems.name: contains_cons_self
theorems.statement: theorem contains_cons_self (val : Int) (xs : List Int) : contains (val :: xs) val = true

lean_translation:
def index_of_max (arr : List Int) : Nat :=
  match arr with
  | [] => 0
  | _ => Id.run do
    let mut idx := 0
    for i in [:arr.length] do
      if arr[i]! > arr[idx]! then idx := i
    return idx

theorems.name: index_of_max_valid
theorems.statement: theorem index_of_max_valid (arr : List Int) (h : arr ≠ []) : index_of_max arr < arr.length

theorems.name: index_of_max_ge_all
theorems.statement: theorem index_of_max_ge_all (arr : List Int) (h : arr ≠ []) : ∀ x ∈ arr, x ≤ arr.get! (index_of_max arr)

source:
def binary_search(arr: list[int], target: int) -> int:
    lo, hi = 0, len(arr) - 1
    while lo <= hi:
        mid = lo + (hi - lo) // 2
        if arr[mid] == target: return mid
        if arr[mid] < target: lo = mid + 1
        else: hi = mid - 1
    return -1
lean_translation:
partial def binary_search (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : Int :=
  let rec loop (lo hi : Int) : Int :=
    if lo > hi then -1
    else
      let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2
      match arr.get? mid.toNat with
      | some x =>
        if x == target then mid
        else if x < target then loop (mid + 1) hi
        else loop lo (mid - 1)
      | none => -1
  loop 0 (arr.length - 1)
tests:
def binary_search(arr, target):
    lo,hi=0,len(arr)-1
    while lo<=hi:
        mid=lo+(hi-lo)//2
        if arr[mid]==target: return mid
        if arr[mid]<target: lo=mid+1
        else: hi=mid-1
    return -1
assert binary_search([1,3,5,7,9],5)==2
assert binary_search([1,3,5,7,9],4)==-1

theorems.name: binary_search_found_valid
theorems.statement: theorem binary_search_found_valid (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (h : binary_search arr target ≠ -1) : 0 ≤ binary_search arr target ∧ binary_search arr target < arr.length

theorems.name: binary_search_nil
theorems.statement: theorem binary_search_nil (target : Int) : binary_search [] target = -1

theorems.name: binary_search_found_eq
theorems.statement: theorem binary_search_found_eq (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (h : binary_search arr target ≠ -1) : arr.get? (binary_search arr target).toNat = some target

source:
def find_first(arr: list[int], target: int) -> int:
    lo, hi, result = 0, len(arr) - 1, -1
    while lo <= hi:
        mid = lo + (hi - lo) // 2
        if arr[mid] == target: result = mid; hi = mid - 1
        elif arr[mid] < target: lo = mid + 1
        else: hi = mid - 1
    return result
lean_translation:
partial def find_first (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : Int :=
  let rec loop (lo hi result : Int) : Int :=
    if lo > hi then result
    else
      let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2
      match arr.get? mid.toNat with
      | some x =>
        if x == target then loop lo (mid - 1) mid
        else if x < target then loop (mid + 1) hi result
        else loop lo (mid - 1) result
      | none => result
  loop 0 (arr.length - 1) (-1)
tests:
def find_first(arr, target):
    lo,hi,r=0,len(arr)-1,-1
    while lo<=hi:
        m=lo+(hi-lo)//2
        if arr[m]==target: r=m; hi=m-1
        elif arr[m]<target: lo=m+1
        else: hi=m-1
    return r
assert find_first([1,2,2,2,3],2)==1
assert find_first([1,2,2,2,3],4)==-1

theorems.name: find_first_le_find_last
theorems.statement: theorem find_first_le_find_last (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : find_first arr target ≤ find_last arr target ∨ find_first arr target = -1

theorems.name: find_first_is_first
theorems.statement: theorem find_first_is_first (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (i : Nat) : find_first arr target ≠ -1 → (i : Int) < find_first arr target → 0 ≤ i → arr.get? i.toNat ≠ some target

def find_last(arr: list[int], target: int) -> int:
    lo, hi, result = 0, len(arr) - 1, -1
    while lo <= hi:
        mid = lo + (hi - lo) // 2
        if arr[mid] == target: result = mid; lo = mid + 1
        elif arr[mid] < target: lo = mid + 1
        else: hi = mid - 1
    return result
lean_translation:
partial def find_last (arr : List Int) (target : Int) : Int :=
  let rec loop (lo hi result : Int) : Int :=
    if lo > hi then result
    else
      let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2
      match arr.get? mid.toNat with
      | some x =>
        if x == target then loop (mid + 1) hi mid
        else if x < target then loop (mid + 1) hi result
        else loop lo (mid - 1) result
      | none => result
  loop 0 (arr.length - 1) (-1)
tests:
def find_last(arr, target):
    lo,hi,r=0,len(arr)-1,-1
    while lo<=hi:
        m=lo+(hi-lo)//2
        if arr[m]==target: r=m; lo=m+1
        elif arr[m]<target: lo=m+1
        else: hi=m-1
    return r
assert find_last([1,2,2,2,3],2)==3
assert find_last([1,2,2,2,3],4)==-1

theorems.name: find_last_is_last
theorems.statement: theorem find_last_is_last (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (i : Int) : find_last arr target ≠ -1 → find_last arr target < i → i < arr.length → arr.get? i.toNat ≠ some target

theorems.name: find_last_found_eq
theorems.statement: theorem find_last_found_eq (arr : List Int) (target : Int) (h : find_last arr target ≠ -1) : arr.get? (find_last arr target).toNat = some target

  arr.any (· == val)

theorems.name: contains_iff_mem
theorems.statement: theorem contains_iff_mem (arr : List Int) (val : Int) : contains arr val = true ↔ val ∈ arr

lean_translation:
def index_of_max (arr : List Int) : Nat :=
  match arr with
  | [] => 0
  | _ => Id.run do
    let mut idx := 0
    for i in [:arr.length] do
      if arr[i]! > arr[idx]! then idx := i
    return idx
tests:
def index_of_max(arr):
    idx=0
    for i in range(1,len(arr)):
        if arr[i]>arr[idx]: idx=i
    return idx
assert index_of_max([1,5,3,7,2])==3
assert index_of_max([10,2,3])==0

theorems.name: index_of_max_valid
theorems.statement: theorem index_of_max_valid (arr : List Int) (h : arr ≠ []) : index_of_max arr < arr.length

theorems.name: index_of_max_ge_all
theorems.statement: theorem index_of_max_ge_all (arr : List Int) (h : arr ≠ []) : ∀ x ∈ arr, x ≤ arr.get! (index_of_max arr)
